title: The EVM gas and execution model
description: Every opcode costs gas the caller prepays; running out reverts all state changes, and storage writes are by far the most expensive operations.
body: Use when reasoning about cost and failure of contract calls. The EVM charges gas per opcode, the sender sets a gas limit and price, and if execution exhausts gas it reverts all state changes (but still consumes the gas); storage writes (SSTORE) cost orders of magnitude more than memory or arithmetic. A transaction is atomic: it fully succeeds or fully reverts. Do not assume unlimited computation or that a partial execution persists. Actions: minimize storage writes, estimate worst-case gas, and keep work under the block gas limit. Validation signals include a gas report and a worst-case-size test fitting the limit. Risk boundary: an operation whose gas grows with attacker-controlled input can become permanently uncallable; treat gas growth as a correctness concern, not just cost.
